method: Bug fix (close_lane.py row[8]→row[9]) + historical repair via artifact= and focus= field recovery

bug.root_cause: Column index off-by-one: row[8]=Tool, row[9]=Scope-Key. close_lane.py copied Tool column value into Scope-Key position.
pollution_mechanism: First closure: Tool value (e.g. claude-code+wsl) placed in Scope-Key position
Merge-on-close removes original ACTIVE row with correct Scope-Key
Subsequent reads find closure row where row[8]=close_lane.py, causing double pollution

findings: L-715 estimated 112/575 (19.5%) pollution — actual is 47/52 (90.4%) of current (post-merge) rows
L-715 undercounted because it measured against historical rows; merge-on-close compacted to 52 rows
artifact= field in Etc is 93.6% recoverable — open_lane.py enforces artifact= which creates recovery path
Bug introduced at tool creation (not a regression) — every close_lane.py invocation was affected
close_lane.py hardcodes Tool column to "close_lane.py" on line 116 — this is by design, but the Scope-Key bug amplified its effect

implications.dependency_graph: Scope-Key layer in swarm_dependency_map.py was 90.4% noise — will now show real tool/file scope per lane
implications.prevention: Bug fix prevents future pollution. open_lane.py --scope-key enforcement provides correct initial value.
implications.data_quality_pattern: artifact= field served as shadow backup for lost Scope-Key — structured Etc fields enable systematic recovery
